## claude.ai bioRxiv
An MCP server for accessing bioRxiv and medRxiv preprint servers.

This server provides:
- Tools: Search preprints, get preprint details, list subject categories
- Prompts: Analyze preprint methodology and findings

bioRxiv is a preprint server for biological sciences operated by Cold Spring
Harbor Laboratory. It hosts research manuscripts that have NOT undergone peer
review. This server wraps the official bioRxiv API to provide structured access
to preprint metadata, abstracts, and full-text PDFs.

TOOL SELECTION GUIDE:
• search_preprints: Find preprints by keyword, author, or date range - primary discovery tool
• get_preprint: Get full details for a specific preprint by DOI
• get_categories: List available subject categories for filtering
• search_published_preprints: Find preprints that have been formally published in journals
• funder_search: Find preprints by funding source (NIH, etc.)
• get_statistics: Usage and submission statistics

MULTI-TOOL WORKFLOWS:
For comprehensive literature analysis:
1. Use search_preprints to find relevant preprints by topic or author
2. Use get_preprint for detailed information on promising results
3. Use search_published_preprints to check if preprints have been peer-reviewed and published
4. Use funder_search to identify research funding patterns in a field

For tracking research trends:
1. Use get_categories to identify relevant subject areas
2. Use search_preprints with date filters for recent activity
3. Use get_statistics to understand submission volumes

This server provides tools to validate, lookup, and search healthcare providers in the
United States National Plan and Provider Enumeration System (NPPES).

The National Provider Identifier (NPI) is a unique 10-digit identifier assigned to healthcare
providers in the United States. There are two types:
- NPI-1 (Individual): Physicians, nurses, dentists, pharmacists, therapists, etc.
- NPI-2 (Organization): Hospitals, clinics, pharmacies, laboratories, nursing homes, etc.

IMPORTANT LIMITATIONS:
- This registry only contains US healthcare providers
- Issuance of an NPI does NOT ensure the provider is licensed or credentialed
- International providers will NOT appear in this database
- API returns maximum 200 results per request, up to 1,200 total across paginated requests
- Data is updated daily from NPPES but may lag behind real-time changes

# Terraform MCP Server Tool Hints

The Terraform MCP server provides tools for generating better Terraform code through registry integration and automating workflows via HCP Terraform/Enterprise APIs.

## Tool Usage Guidelines

**BEFORE generating any Terraform code**: Query registries for latest provider/module versions and styling guidelines. When enterprise tools are enabled AND a Terraform token is provided, search private registries first, then fall back to public.

**Validation Flow**: Run terraform validate immediately after generation, then terraform plan only if validation passes. Use terraform fmt to format code as needed.

**User Confirmation Required**: ALWAYS get explicit yes/no confirmation before: `create_run`, `apply_run`, `discard_run`, `cancel_run`.

- **Module Discovery**: `get_latest_module_version` (if unavailable in code) → `search_modules` → `get_module_details`

- **Policy Discovery**: `search_policies` → `get_policy_details`

- Use these to ensure generated code uses current versions and follows best practices

content.content:
1	# Publish Homebrew cask — called by release.yml after a tagged release
2	# is live on GitHub.
3	#
4	# Writes an updated Cask formula to the external tap repository
5	# `dougnukem/homebrew-orpheus` so users can `brew install --cask orpheus`
6	# and receive the newest version.
7	#
8	# Requires:
9	#   - a HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN secret (GitHub PAT with repo scope on the tap)
10	#   - the tap repo to already exist and contain Casks/orpheus.rb (even
11	#     empty). The first version is bootstrapped by hand; subsequent
12	#     releases are updated automatically by this workflow.
13	#
14	# macOS signing status:
15	#   * The Tauri .app produced by release.yml is currently UNSIGNED. The
16	#     cask installs successfully but users hit Gatekeeper on first launch.
17	#   * The cask template below includes Gatekeeper-bypass instructions in
18	#     its `caveats` block.
19	#   * Once Apple Developer signing is enabled in release.yml, delete the
20	#     caveats block in packaging/homebrew/orpheus.rb.template and re-run.
21	
22	name: publish-cask
23	
24	on:
25	  workflow_call:
26	    inputs:
27	      tag:
28	        description: "Release tag (e.g. v0.1.1)."
29	        required: true
30	        type: string
31	  workflow_dispatch:
32	    inputs:
33	      tag:
34	        description: "Release tag (e.g. v0.1.1)."
35	        required: true
36	        type: string
37	
38	permissions:
39	  contents: read
40	
41	concurrency:
42	  group: publish-cask-${{ inputs.tag }}
43	  cancel-in-progress: false
44	
45	env:
46	  TAP_OWNER: dougnukem
47	  TAP_REPO: homebrew-orpheus
48	
49	jobs:
50	  update-cask:
51	    name: update homebrew cask
52	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
53	    env:
54	      TAG: ${{ inputs.tag }}
55	      REPO: ${{ github.repository }}
56	    steps:
57	      - name: check HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN is set
58	        id: gate
59	        env:
60	          TAP_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN }}
61	        shell: bash
62	        run: |
63	          if [[ -z "$TAP_TOKEN" ]]; then
64	            echo "::warning::HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN not set; skipping cask publish"
65	            echo "skip=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
66	          else
67	            echo "skip=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
68	          fi
69	
70	      - if: steps.gate.outputs.skip == 'false'
71	        uses: actions/checkout@v4
72	        with:
73	          ref: ${{ inputs.tag }}
74	
75	      - if: steps.gate.outputs.skip == 'false'
76	        name: derive version
77	        id: ver
78	        shell: bash
79	        run: |
80	          set -euo pipefail
81	          VERSION="${TAG#v}"
82	          echo "version=$VERSION" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
83	
84	      - if: steps.gate.outputs.skip == 'false'
85	        name: compute artifact sha256s
86	        id: sha
87	        env:
88	          VERSION: ${{ steps.ver.outputs.version }}
89	        shell: bash
90	        run: |
91	          set -euo pipefail
92	          BASE="https://github.com/$REPO/releases/download/$TAG"
93	          # Tauri produces per-arch DMGs named like:
94	          #   Orpheus_0.1.0_aarch64.dmg
95	          #   Orpheus_0.1.0_x64.dmg
96	          # The universal-apple-darwin target combines both architectures
97	          # into a single DMG labelled universal. Fetch whichever exists.
98	          fetch_sha() {
99	            local url=$1
100	            if curl -sfL -o /tmp/art "$url"; then
101	              sha256sum /tmp/art | cut -d' ' -f1
102	            else
103	              echo "MISSING"
104	            fi
105	          }
106	          ARM_SHA=$(fetch_sha "$BASE/Orpheus_${VERSION}_aarch64.dmg")
107	          INTEL_SHA=$(fetch_sha "$BASE/Orpheus_${VERSION}_x64.dmg")
108	          UNIV_SHA=$(fetch_sha "$BASE/Orpheus_${VERSION}_universal.dmg")
109	          echo "arm=$ARM_SHA"        >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
110	          echo "intel=$INTEL_SHA"    >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
111	          echo "universal=$UNIV_SHA" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
112	
113	      - if: steps.gate.outputs.skip == 'false'
114	        name: checkout tap repo
115	        uses: actions/checkout@v4
116	        with:
117	          repository: ${{ env.TAP_OWNER }}/${{ env.TAP_REPO }}
118	          token: ${{ secrets.HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN }}
119	          path: tap
120	
121	      - if: steps.gate.outputs.skip == 'false'
122	        name: render cask from template
123	        env:
124	          VERSION: ${{ steps.ver.outputs.version }}
125	          ARM_SHA: ${{ steps.sha.outputs.arm }}
126	          INTEL_SHA: ${{ steps.sha.outputs.intel }}
127	          UNIV_SHA: ${{ steps.sha.outputs.universal }}
128	        shell: bash
129	        run: |
130	          set -euo pipefail
131	          mkdir -p tap/Casks
132	          if [[ "$UNIV_SHA" != "MISSING" ]]; then
133	            # Single-SHA universal binary variant.
134	            sed \
135	              -e "s|%%VERSION%%|$VERSION|g" \
136	              -e "s|%%REPO%%|$REPO|g" \
137	              -e "s|%%UNIVERSAL_SHA%%|$UNIV_SHA|g" \
138	              packaging/homebrew/orpheus.rb.universal.template > tap/Casks/orpheus.rb
139	          else
140	            if [[ "$ARM_SHA" == "MISSING" || "$INTEL_SHA" == "MISSING" ]]; then
141	              echo "::error::neither universal nor both per-arch DMGs present for $TAG"
142	              exit 1
143	            fi
144	            sed \
145	              -e "s|%%VERSION%%|$VERSION|g" \
146	              -e "s|%%REPO%%|$REPO|g" \
147	              -e "s|%%ARM_SHA%%|$ARM_SHA|g" \
148	              -e "s|%%INTEL_SHA%%|$INTEL_SHA|g" \
149	              packaging/homebrew/orpheus.rb.perarch.template > tap/Casks/orpheus.rb
150	          fi
151	          cat tap/Casks/orpheus.rb
152	
153	      - if: steps.gate.outputs.skip == 'false'
154	        name: commit + push tap update
155	        env:
156	          VERSION: ${{ steps.ver.outputs.version }}
157	        shell: bash
158	        working-directory: tap
159	        run: |
160	          set -euo pipefail
161	          if git diff --quiet; then
162	            echo "cask already up to date; nothing to commit"
163	            exit 0
164	          fi
165	          git config user.name "orpheus-release-bot"
166	          git config user.email "bot@users.noreply.github.com"
167	          git add Casks/orpheus.rb
168	          git commit -m "orpheus ${VERSION}"
169	          git push
170

# and receive the newest version.
#
# Requires:
#   - a HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN secret (GitHub PAT with repo scope on the tap)
#   - the tap repo to already exist and contain Casks/orpheus.rb (even
#     empty). The first version is bootstrapped by hand; subsequent
#     releases are updated automatically by this workflow.
#
# macOS signing status:
#   * The Tauri .app produced by release.yml is currently UNSIGNED. The
#     cask installs successfully but users hit Gatekeeper on first launch.
#   * The cask template below includes Gatekeeper-bypass instructions in
#     its `caveats` block.
#   * Once Apple Developer signing is enabled in release.yml, delete the
#     caveats block in packaging/homebrew/orpheus.rb.template and re-run.

name: publish-cask

on:
  workflow_call:
    inputs:
      tag:
        description: "Release tag (e.g. v0.1.1)."
        required: true
        type: string
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      tag:
        description: "Release tag (e.g. v0.1.1)."
        required: true
        type: string

permissions:
  contents: read

concurrency:
  group: publish-cask-${{ inputs.tag }}
  cancel-in-progress: false

env:
  TAP_OWNER: dougnukem
  TAP_REPO: homebrew-orpheus

jobs:
  update-cask:
    name: update homebrew cask
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    env:
      TAG: ${{ inputs.tag }}
      REPO: ${{ github.repository }}
    steps:
      - name: check HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN is set
        id: gate
        env:
          TAP_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN }}
        shell: bash
        run: |
          if [[ -z "$TAP_TOKEN" ]]; then
            echo "::warning::HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN not set; skipping cask publish"
            echo "skip=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          else
            echo "skip=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          fi

      - if: steps.gate.outputs.skip == 'false'
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          ref: ${{ inputs.tag }}

      - if: steps.gate.outputs.skip == 'false'
        name: derive version
        id: ver
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          VERSION="${TAG#v}"
          echo "version=$VERSION" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - if: steps.gate.outputs.skip == 'false'
        name: compute artifact sha256s
        id: sha
        env:
          VERSION: ${{ steps.ver.outputs.version }}
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          BASE="https://github.com/$REPO/releases/download/$TAG"
          # Tauri produces per-arch DMGs named like:
          #   Orpheus_0.1.0_aarch64.dmg
          #   Orpheus_0.1.0_x64.dmg
          # The universal-apple-darwin target combines both architectures
          # into a single DMG labelled universal. Fetch whichever exists.
          fetch_sha() {
            local url=$1
            if curl -sfL -o /tmp/art "$url"; then
              sha256sum /tmp/art | cut -d' ' -f1
            else
              echo "MISSING"
            fi
          }
          ARM_SHA=$(fetch_sha "$BASE/Orpheus_${VERSION}_aarch64.dmg")
          INTEL_SHA=$(fetch_sha "$BASE/Orpheus_${VERSION}_x64.dmg")
          UNIV_SHA=$(fetch_sha "$BASE/Orpheus_${VERSION}_universal.dmg")
          echo "arm=$ARM_SHA"        >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "intel=$INTEL_SHA"    >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "universal=$UNIV_SHA" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - if: steps.gate.outputs.skip == 'false'
        name: checkout tap repo
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          repository: ${{ env.TAP_OWNER }}/${{ env.TAP_REPO }}
          token: ${{ secrets.HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN }}
          path: tap

      - if: steps.gate.outputs.skip == 'false'
        name: render cask from template
        env:
          VERSION: ${{ steps.ver.outputs.version }}
          ARM_SHA: ${{ steps.sha.outputs.arm }}
          INTEL_SHA: ${{ steps.sha.outputs.intel }}
          UNIV_SHA: ${{ steps.sha.outputs.universal }}
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          mkdir -p tap/Casks
          if [[ "$UNIV_SHA" != "MISSING" ]]; then
            # Single-SHA universal binary variant.
            sed \
              -e "s|%%VERSION%%|$VERSION|g" \
              -e "s|%%REPO%%|$REPO|g" \
              -e "s|%%UNIVERSAL_SHA%%|$UNIV_SHA|g" \
              packaging/homebrew/orpheus.rb.universal.template > tap/Casks/orpheus.rb
          else
            if [[ "$ARM_SHA" == "MISSING" || "$INTEL_SHA" == "MISSING" ]]; then
              echo "::error::neither universal nor both per-arch DMGs present for $TAG"
              exit 1
            fi
            sed \
              -e "s|%%VERSION%%|$VERSION|g" \
              -e "s|%%REPO%%|$REPO|g" \
              -e "s|%%ARM_SHA%%|$ARM_SHA|g" \
              -e "s|%%INTEL_SHA%%|$INTEL_SHA|g" \
              packaging/homebrew/orpheus.rb.perarch.template > tap/Casks/orpheus.rb
          fi
          cat tap/Casks/orpheus.rb

      - if: steps.gate.outputs.skip == 'false'
        name: commit + push tap update
        env:
          VERSION: ${{ steps.ver.outputs.version }}
        shell: bash
        working-directory: tap
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          if git diff --quiet; then
            echo "cask already up to date; nothing to commit"
            exit 0
          fi
          git config user.name "orpheus-release-bot"
          git config user.email "bot@users.noreply.github.com"
          git add Casks/orpheus.rb
          git commit -m "orpheus ${VERSION}"
          git push

content.text:
There isn't a HEAD-tracking cask — [publish-cask.yml](.github/workflows/publish-cask.yml) only fires from tagged `release.yml` runs, so `dougnukem/homebrew-orpheus` is frozen at the last tag. The `latest` prerelease (rebuilt on every push to `main` by [latest.yml](.github/workflows/latest.yml)) ships `Orpheus_0.1.0_universal.dmg` but no cask update.

Two practical options:

**1. One-shot local cask pointing at `latest`** — save as `~/orpheus-head.rb`:

```ruby
cask "orpheus-head" do
  version :latest
  sha256 :no_check

  url "https://github.com/dougnukem/orpheus/releases/download/latest/Orpheus_0.1.0_universal.dmg"
  name "Orpheus (HEAD)"
  homepage "https://github.com/dougnukem/orpheus"

  app "Orpheus.app"
  caveats "xattr -cr /Applications/Orpheus.app   # unsigned; clears Gatekeeper"
end
```

Then:
```bash
brew install --cask ~/orpheus-head.rb
xattr -cr /Applications/Orpheus.app
```
Reinstall with `brew reinstall --cask ~/orpheus-head.rb` to pick up a newer HEAD (SHA is unchecked, so brew won't fight you).

**2. Skip brew, install the DMG directly:**
```bash
curl -fL -o /tmp/orpheus.dmg \
  https://github.com/dougnukem/orpheus/releases/download/latest/Orpheus_0.1.0_universal.dmg
hdiutil attach /tmp/orpheus.dmg
cp -R "/Volumes/Orpheus/Orpheus.app" /Applications/
hdiutil detach "/Volumes/Orpheus"
xattr -cr /Applications/Orpheus.app
```

If you want a proper `brew install --cask orpheus@head` flow, we'd need to add a `publish-cask-head` job to [latest.yml](.github/workflows/latest.yml) that writes a second cask (`orpheus-head.rb`) into the tap with `version :latest, sha256 :no_check`. Want me to wire that up?

47	            archive: tar.gz

49	          # GitHub's macos-13 (Intel) runner pool is effectively unavailable
50	          # (jobs queue for hours and never start). `rustup target add` +
51	          # `cargo build --target x86_64-apple-darwin` on macos-latest
52	          # produces the same artifact.
53	          - target: x86_64-apple-darwin

57	          - target: aarch64-apple-darwin

78	      - uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2

91	          cp "target/$TARGET/release/orpheus" "staging/$ARTIFACT/"

93	          cp README.md LICENSE "staging/$ARTIFACT/"

106	      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v7
107	        with:

113	    name: tauri / ${{ matrix.name }}
114	    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
115	    strategy:

121	            args: --target universal-apple-darwin

123	          - name: linux-x86_64

130	            rust-targets: ""

132	      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
133	      - uses: jdx/mise-action@v4
134	        with:
135	          experimental: true
136	          install: true
137	          cache: true
138	      - name: add rust targets
139	        if: matrix.rust-targets != ''
140	        shell: bash
141	        env:
142	          RUST_TARGETS: ${{ matrix.rust-targets }}
143	        run: |
144	          IFS=',' read -r -a targets <<< "$RUST_TARGETS"
145	          for t in "${targets[@]}"; do rustup target add "$t"; done
146	      - uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
147	        with:
148	          workspaces: "crates/orpheus-tauri/src-tauri -> target"
149	          shared-key: latest-tauri-${{ matrix.name }}
150	
151	      - uses: ./.github/actions/install-linux-deps
152	
153	      - run: mise run web:install
154	      - uses: tauri-apps/tauri-action@v0
155	        env:
156	          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
157	        with:
158	          projectPath: crates/orpheus-tauri/src-tauri
159	          args: ${{ matrix.args }}
160	
161	      - name: collect tauri bundles
162	        shell: bash
163	        run: |
164	          set -euo pipefail
165	          mkdir -p tauri-output
166	          # Cargo workspace: bundles land under the workspace root `target/`.
167	          # Layout varies by target triple:
168	          #   native   -> target/release/bundle/<kind>/...
169	          #   crossed  -> target/<triple>/release/bundle/<kind>/...
170	          #               (e.g. target/universal-apple-darwin/release/bundle/...)
171	          # So search the whole target/ tree and filter by the
172	          # .../release/bundle/ path segment. Tauri v2 emits duplicates
173	          # (Orpheus_0.1.0_amd64.deb AND lowercase
174	          # orpheus_0.1.0_amd64.deb from the Cargo crate name); the

178	            \( -name "Orpheus_*.dmg"    -o -name "Orpheus*.app.tar.gz" \
179	               -o -name "Orpheus_*.AppImage" \
180	               -o -name "Orpheus_*.deb" -o -name "Orpheus-*.rpm" \
181	               -o -name "Orpheus_*.msi" -o -name "Orpheus_*.exe" \) \
182	            -exec cp {} tauri-output/ \;
183	          ls -la tauri-output/
184	          if [ -z "$(ls -A tauri-output/)" ]; then
185	            echo "::error::no Tauri bundles found under target/**/release/bundle"
186	            find target -type d -name bundle 2>&1 | head -20 || true
187	            exit 1
188	          fi
189	
190	      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v7
191	        with:
192	          name: tauri-${{ matrix.name }}
193	          path: tauri-output/*
194	          if-no-files-found: error

196	  publish-latest:
197	    name: publish latest pre-release
198	    needs: [build-cli, build-tauri]
199	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

213	          merge-multiple: true

218	      - name: sign artifacts with gpg

220	        with:
221	          artifacts-dir: dist
222	          gpg-private-key: ${{ secrets.GPG_PRIVATE_KEY }}
223	          gpg-passphrase: ${{ secrets.GPG_PASSPHRASE }}
224	          gpg-key-id: ${{ secrets.GPG_KEY_ID }}
225	
226	      - name: delete previous latest release
227	        env:
228	          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

235	          name: "Latest (main @ ${{ github.sha }})"
236	          prerelease: true
237	          make_latest: "false"
238	          body: |
239	            **Rolling pre-release built from `main`.** Do not depend on it for
240	            reproducible builds — it changes on every push. For a stable
241	            version, use a tagged release.
242	
243	            - commit: `${{ github.sha }}`
244	            - run:    `${{ github.run_id }}`
245	
246	            All artifacts are GPG-signed. Verify with:
247	            ```
248	            gpg --verify SHA256SUMS.asc SHA256SUMS
249	            sha256sum -c SHA256SUMS
250	            ```
251	          files: dist/*
252	          fail_on_unmatched_files: true

#   1. Build CLI + server binaries across native-runner matrix.
#   2. Build Tauri desktop bundles via tauri-action.

name: latest

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  workflow_dispatch:

permissions:
  contents: read

concurrency:
  group: latest
  cancel-in-progress: true

env:
  CARGO_TERM_COLOR: always
  LATEST_TAG: latest

jobs:
  build-cli:
    name: cli / ${{ matrix.target }}
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        include:
          - target: x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu
            os: ubuntu-latest
            artifact: orpheus-linux-x86_64
            archive: tar.gz
          - target: aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu
            os: ubuntu-24.04-arm
            artifact: orpheus-linux-aarch64
            archive: tar.gz
          # Cross-compile x86_64 binaries from the Apple Silicon runner —
          # GitHub's macos-13 (Intel) runner pool is effectively unavailable
          # (jobs queue for hours and never start). `rustup target add` +
          # `cargo build --target x86_64-apple-darwin` on macos-latest
          # produces the same artifact.
          - target: x86_64-apple-darwin
            os: macos-latest
            artifact: orpheus-macos-x86_64
            archive: tar.gz
          - target: aarch64-apple-darwin
            os: macos-latest
            artifact: orpheus-macos-aarch64
            archive: tar.gz
          - target: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc
            os: windows-latest
            artifact: orpheus-windows-x86_64
            archive: zip
    env:
      TARGET: ${{ matrix.target }}
      ARTIFACT: ${{ matrix.artifact }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
      - uses: jdx/mise-action@v4
        with:
          experimental: true
          install: true
          cache: true
      - name: add rust target
        shell: bash
        run: rustup target add "$TARGET"
      - uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
        with:
          shared-key: latest-cli-${{ matrix.target }}

      - name: build release binaries
        shell: bash
        run: cargo build --release --locked --target "$TARGET" -p orpheus-cli -p orpheus-server

      - name: stage artifact (unix)
        if: runner.os != 'Windows'
        shell: bash
        run: |
          mkdir -p "staging/$ARTIFACT"
          cp "target/$TARGET/release/orpheus" "staging/$ARTIFACT/"
          cp "target/$TARGET/release/orpheus-server" "staging/$ARTIFACT/"
          cp README.md LICENSE "staging/$ARTIFACT/"
          ( cd staging && tar -czf "$ARTIFACT.tar.gz" "$ARTIFACT" )

      - name: stage artifact (windows)
        if: runner.os == 'Windows'
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path "staging/$env:ARTIFACT"
          Copy-Item "target/$env:TARGET/release/orpheus.exe" "staging/$env:ARTIFACT/"
          Copy-Item "target/$env:TARGET/release/orpheus-server.exe" "staging/$env:ARTIFACT/"
          Copy-Item README.md, LICENSE "staging/$env:ARTIFACT/"
          Compress-Archive -Path "staging/$env:ARTIFACT/*" -DestinationPath "staging/$env:ARTIFACT.zip"

      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v7
        with:
          name: ${{ matrix.artifact }}
          path: staging/${{ matrix.artifact }}.${{ matrix.archive }}
          if-no-files-found: error

  build-tauri:
    name: tauri / ${{ matrix.name }}
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        include:
          - name: macos-universal
            os: macos-latest
            args: --target universal-apple-darwin
            rust-targets: aarch64-apple-darwin,x86_64-apple-darwin
          - name: linux-x86_64
            os: ubuntu-22.04
            args: ""
            rust-targets: ""
          - name: windows-x86_64
            os: windows-latest
            args: ""
            rust-targets: ""
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
      - uses: jdx/mise-action@v4
        with:
          experimental: true
          install: true
          cache: true
      - name: add rust targets
        if: matrix.rust-targets != ''
        shell: bash
        env:
          RUST_TARGETS: ${{ matrix.rust-targets }}
        run: |
          IFS=',' read -r -a targets <<< "$RUST_TARGETS"
          for t in "${targets[@]}"; do rustup target add "$t"; done
      - uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
        with:
          workspaces: "crates/orpheus-tauri/src-tauri -> target"
          shared-key: latest-tauri-${{ matrix.name }}

      - uses: ./.github/actions/install-linux-deps

      - run: mise run web:install
      - uses: tauri-apps/tauri-action@v0
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        with:
          projectPath: crates/orpheus-tauri/src-tauri
          args: ${{ matrix.args }}

      - name: collect tauri bundles
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          mkdir -p tauri-output
          # Cargo workspace: bundles land under the workspace root `target/`.
          # Layout varies by target triple:
          #   native   -> target/release/bundle/<kind>/...
          #   crossed  -> target/<triple>/release/bundle/<kind>/...
          #               (e.g. target/universal-apple-darwin/release/bundle/...)
          # So search the whole target/ tree and filter by the
          # .../release/bundle/ path segment. Tauri v2 emits duplicates
          # (Orpheus_0.1.0_amd64.deb AND lowercase
          # orpheus_0.1.0_amd64.deb from the Cargo crate name); the
          # Orpheus_/Orpheus- name filter keeps only the productName-cased
          # ones.
          find target -type f -path "*/release/bundle/*" \
            \( -name "Orpheus_*.dmg"    -o -name "Orpheus*.app.tar.gz" \
               -o -name "Orpheus_*.AppImage" \
               -o -name "Orpheus_*.deb" -o -name "Orpheus-*.rpm" \
               -o -name "Orpheus_*.msi" -o -name "Orpheus_*.exe" \) \
            -exec cp {} tauri-output/ \;
          ls -la tauri-output/
          if [ -z "$(ls -A tauri-output/)" ]; then
            echo "::error::no Tauri bundles found under target/**/release/bundle"
            find target -type d -name bundle 2>&1 | head -20 || true
            exit 1
          fi

      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v7
        with:
          name: tauri-${{ matrix.name }}
          path: tauri-output/*
          if-no-files-found: error

  publish-latest:
    name: publish latest pre-release
    needs: [build-cli, build-tauri]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write
    env:
      SHA: ${{ github.sha }}
      RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - uses: actions/download-artifact@v8
        with:
          path: dist
          merge-multiple: true

      - name: list artifacts
        run: ls -la dist/

      - name: sign artifacts with gpg
        uses: ./.github/actions/sign-artifacts
        with:
          artifacts-dir: dist
          gpg-private-key: ${{ secrets.GPG_PRIVATE_KEY }}
          gpg-passphrase: ${{ secrets.GPG_PASSPHRASE }}
          gpg-key-id: ${{ secrets.GPG_KEY_ID }}

      - name: delete previous latest release
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        run: gh release delete "$LATEST_TAG" --yes --cleanup-tag || true

      - name: create latest pre-release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v3
        with:
          tag_name: ${{ env.LATEST_TAG }}
          name: "Latest (main @ ${{ github.sha }})"
          prerelease: true
          make_latest: "false"
          body: |
            **Rolling pre-release built from `main`.** Do not depend on it for
            reproducible builds — it changes on every push. For a stable
            version, use a tagged release.

            - commit: `${{ github.sha }}`
            - run:    `${{ github.run_id }}`

            All artifacts are GPG-signed. Verify with:
            ```
            gpg --verify SHA256SUMS.asc SHA256SUMS
            sha256sum -c SHA256SUMS
            ```
          files: dist/*
          fail_on_unmatched_files: true

# Homebrew cask templates

These templates are rendered by `.github/workflows/publish-cask.yml` into
the external tap repo `dougnukem/homebrew-orpheus` on each tagged release.

- `orpheus.rb.universal.template` — used when a single universal DMG
  (`Orpheus_<ver>_universal.dmg`) is present on the release. This is the
  output of `tauri build --target universal-apple-darwin`.
- `orpheus.rb.perarch.template` — fallback when per-architecture DMGs
  (`Orpheus_<ver>_aarch64.dmg`, `Orpheus_<ver>_x64.dmg`) are present
  instead. Useful if the universal target is disabled.

Token substitution:

| Token              | Meaning                                             |
|--------------------|-----------------------------------------------------|
| `%%VERSION%%`      | Release version without the leading `v` (e.g. `0.1.0`) |
| `%%REPO%%`         | `<owner>/<repo>` of the app repo                    |
| `%%UNIVERSAL_SHA%%`| SHA-256 of the universal DMG                        |
| `%%ARM_SHA%%`      | SHA-256 of the arm64 DMG                            |
| `%%INTEL_SHA%%`    | SHA-256 of the x86_64 DMG                           |

## Bootstrapping the tap

The first time, create the tap repo by hand:

```bash
gh repo create dougnukem/homebrew-orpheus --public --add-readme \
  --description "Homebrew tap for Orpheus"
cd homebrew-orpheus
mkdir Casks
# seed with a placeholder the workflow will overwrite
printf 'cask "orpheus" do\n  version "0.0.0"\nend\n' > Casks/orpheus.rb
git add . && git commit -m "init tap" && git push
```

Users then install with:

```bash
brew tap dougnukem/orpheus
brew install --cask orpheus
```

## Removing the Gatekeeper caveat

Once Apple Developer signing is wired up in `release.yml`, delete the
`caveats <<~EOS ... EOS` block in both templates. The cask will then
install and launch cleanly with no manual `xattr` step.

input.content:
# Publish Homebrew HEAD cask — called by latest.yml after the rolling
# `latest` pre-release is atomically replaced on every push to main.
#
# Writes `Casks/orpheus-head.rb` to the external tap repo
# `dougnukem/homebrew-orpheus` so users can
# `brew install --cask dougnukem/orpheus/orpheus-head` and receive a
# fresh rolling build with every `brew reinstall --cask orpheus-head`.
#
# The head cask uses `version :latest` + `sha256 :no_check` because the
# artifact hash changes on every push to main. Users opt into this
# instability explicitly by installing the `-head` variant.
#
# Requires:
#   - HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN secret (GitHub PAT with repo scope on the tap)
#   - the tap repo to already exist (bootstrapped by publish-cask.yml on
#     the first tagged release). If it does not exist yet this workflow
#     will fail — run publish-cask.yml first, or create the repo by hand.

name: publish-cask-head

on:
  workflow_call: {}
  workflow_dispatch: {}

permissions:
  contents: read

concurrency:
  group: publish-cask-head
  cancel-in-progress: false

env:
  TAP_OWNER: dougnukem
  TAP_REPO: homebrew-orpheus
  LATEST_TAG: latest

jobs:
  update-cask-head:
    name: update homebrew head cask
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    env:
      REPO: ${{ github.repository }}
      SHA: ${{ github.sha }}
    steps:
      - name: check HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN is set
        id: gate
        env:
          TAP_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN }}
        shell: bash
        run: |
          if [[ -z "$TAP_TOKEN" ]]; then
            echo "::warning::HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN not set; skipping head cask publish"
            echo "skip=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          else
            echo "skip=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          fi

      - if: steps.gate.outputs.skip == 'false'
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - if: steps.gate.outputs.skip == 'false'
        name: locate universal dmg on latest release
        id: dmg
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          DMG=$(gh release view "$LATEST_TAG" --repo "$REPO" \
            --json assets --jq '.assets[].name' \
            | grep -E '^Orpheus_.*_universal\.dmg$' \
            | head -n1 || true)
          if [[ -z "$DMG" ]]; then
            echo "::error::no Orpheus_*_universal.dmg asset on $LATEST_TAG release"
            exit 1
          fi
          echo "dmg=$DMG" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - if: steps.gate.outputs.skip == 'false'
        name: checkout tap repo
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          repository: ${{ env.TAP_OWNER }}/${{ env.TAP_REPO }}
          token: ${{ secrets.HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN }}
          path: tap

      - if: steps.gate.outputs.skip == 'false'
        name: render head cask from template
        env:
          DMG: ${{ steps.dmg.outputs.dmg }}
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          mkdir -p tap/Casks
          sed \
            -e "s|%%REPO%%|$REPO|g" \
            -e "s|%%DMG_FILENAME%%|$DMG|g" \
            -e "s|%%TAP_OWNER%%|$TAP_OWNER|g" \
            -e "s|%%TAP_NAME%%|orpheus|g" \
            packaging/homebrew/orpheus-head.rb.template > tap/Casks/orpheus-head.rb
          cat tap/Casks/orpheus-head.rb

      - if: steps.gate.outputs.skip == 'false'
        name: commit + push tap update
        shell: bash
        working-directory: tap
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          if git diff --quiet; then
            echo "head cask already up to date; nothing to commit"
            exit 0
          fi
          git config user.name "orpheus-release-bot"
          git config user.email "bot@users.noreply.github.com"
          git add Casks/orpheus-head.rb
          git commit -m "orpheus-head @ ${SHA:0:7}"
          git push

# Publish Homebrew HEAD cask — called by latest.yml after the rolling
# `latest` pre-release is atomically replaced on every push to main.
#
# Writes `Casks/orpheus-head.rb` to the external tap repo
# `dougnukem/homebrew-orpheus` so users can
# `brew install --cask dougnukem/orpheus/orpheus-head` and receive a
# fresh rolling build with every `brew reinstall --cask orpheus-head`.
#
# The head cask uses `version :latest` + `sha256 :no_check` because the
# artifact hash changes on every push to main. Users opt into this
# instability explicitly by installing the `-head` variant.
#
# Requires:
#   - HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN secret (GitHub PAT with repo scope on the tap)
#   - the tap repo to already exist (bootstrapped by publish-cask.yml on
#     the first tagged release). If it does not exist yet this workflow
#     will fail — run publish-cask.yml first, or create the repo by hand.
#
# Security: all ${{ github.* }} inputs are routed through env: blocks and
# referenced as shell variables. The workflow only uses trusted context
# values (github.repository, github.sha) — no untrusted event payload.

name: publish-cask-head

on:
  workflow_call: {}
  workflow_dispatch: {}

permissions:
  contents: read

concurrency:
  group: publish-cask-head
  cancel-in-progress: false

env:
  TAP_OWNER: dougnukem
  TAP_REPO: homebrew-orpheus
  LATEST_TAG: latest

jobs:
  update-cask-head:
    name: update homebrew head cask
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    env:
      REPO: ${{ github.repository }}
      SHA: ${{ github.sha }}
    steps:
      - name: check HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN is set
        id: gate
        env:
          TAP_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN }}
        shell: bash
        run: |
          if [[ -z "$TAP_TOKEN" ]]; then
            echo "::warning::HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN not set; skipping head cask publish"
            echo "skip=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          else
            echo "skip=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          fi

      - if: steps.gate.outputs.skip == 'false'
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - if: steps.gate.outputs.skip == 'false'
        name: locate universal dmg on latest release
        id: dmg
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          DMG=$(gh release view "$LATEST_TAG" --repo "$REPO" \
            --json assets --jq '.assets[].name' \
            | grep -E '^Orpheus_.*_universal\.dmg$' \
            | head -n1 || true)
          if [[ -z "$DMG" ]]; then
            echo "::error::no Orpheus_*_universal.dmg asset on $LATEST_TAG release"
            exit 1
          fi
          echo "dmg=$DMG" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - if: steps.gate.outputs.skip == 'false'
        name: checkout tap repo
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          repository: ${{ env.TAP_OWNER }}/${{ env.TAP_REPO }}
          token: ${{ secrets.HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN }}
          path: tap

      - if: steps.gate.outputs.skip == 'false'
        name: render head cask from template
        env:
          DMG: ${{ steps.dmg.outputs.dmg }}
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          mkdir -p tap/Casks
          sed \
            -e "s|%%REPO%%|$REPO|g" \
            -e "s|%%DMG_FILENAME%%|$DMG|g" \
            -e "s|%%TAP_OWNER%%|$TAP_OWNER|g" \
            -e "s|%%TAP_NAME%%|orpheus|g" \
            packaging/homebrew/orpheus-head.rb.template > tap/Casks/orpheus-head.rb
          cat tap/Casks/orpheus-head.rb

      - if: steps.gate.outputs.skip == 'false'
        name: commit + push tap update
        shell: bash
        working-directory: tap
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          if git diff --quiet; then
            echo "head cask already up to date; nothing to commit"
            exit 0
          fi
          git config user.name "orpheus-release-bot"
          git config user.email "bot@users.noreply.github.com"
          git add Casks/orpheus-head.rb
          git commit -m "orpheus-head @ ${SHA:0:7}"
          git push

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Latest — continuous pre-release tracking `main`.
#
# On every push to main:
#   1. Build CLI + server binaries across native-runner matrix.
#   2. Build Tauri desktop bundles via tauri-action.
#   3. Atomically publish/replace the `latest` PRE-release on GitHub with
#      every artifact attached. The `latest` release is marked prerelease
#      so GitHub's "Latest release" pointer never tracks it — that pointer
#      is reserved for tagged releases created by release.yml.
#
# `${{ github.sha }}` etc. are bound into the job via env: blocks and then
# referenced as shell variables to neutralise any scripting-injection risk.

name: latest

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  workflow_dispatch:

permissions:
  contents: read

concurrency:
  group: latest
  cancel-in-progress: true

env:
  CARGO_TERM_COLOR: always
  LATEST_TAG: latest

jobs:
  build-cli:
    name: cli / ${{ matrix.target }}
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        include:
          - target: x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu
            os: ubuntu-latest
            artifact: orpheus-linux-x86_64
            archive: tar.gz
          - target: aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu
            os: ubuntu-24.04-arm
            artifact: orpheus-linux-aarch64
            archive: tar.gz
          # Cross-compile x86_64 binaries from the Apple Silicon runner —
          # GitHub's macos-13 (Intel) runner pool is effectively unavailable
          # (jobs queue for hours and never start). `rustup target add` +
          # `cargo build --target x86_64-apple-darwin` on macos-latest
          # produces the same artifact.
          - target: x86_64-apple-darwin
            os: macos-latest
            artifact: orpheus-macos-x86_64
            archive: tar.gz
          - target: aarch64-apple-darwin
            os: macos-latest
            artifact: orpheus-macos-aarch64
            archive: tar.gz
          - target: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc
            os: windows-latest
            artifact: orpheus-windows-x86_64
            archive: zip
    env:
      TARGET: ${{ matrix.target }}
      ARTIFACT: ${{ matrix.artifact }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
      - uses: jdx/mise-action@v4
        with:
          experimental: true
          install: true
          cache: true
      - name: add rust target
        shell: bash
        run: rustup target add "$TARGET"
      - uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
        with:
          shared-key: latest-cli-${{ matrix.target }}

      - name: build release binaries
        shell: bash
        run: cargo build --release --locked --target "$TARGET" -p orpheus-cli -p orpheus-server

      - name: stage artifact (unix)
        if: runner.os != 'Windows'
        shell: bash
        run: |
          mkdir -p "staging/$ARTIFACT"
          cp "target/$TARGET/release/orpheus" "staging/$ARTIFACT/"
          cp "target/$TARGET/release/orpheus-server" "staging/$ARTIFACT/"
          cp README.md LICENSE "staging/$ARTIFACT/"
          ( cd staging && tar -czf "$ARTIFACT.tar.gz" "$ARTIFACT" )

      - name: stage artifact (windows)
        if: runner.os == 'Windows'
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path "staging/$env:ARTIFACT"
          Copy-Item "target/$env:TARGET/release/orpheus.exe" "staging/$env:ARTIFACT/"
          Copy-Item "target/$env:TARGET/release/orpheus-server.exe" "staging/$env:ARTIFACT/"
          Copy-Item README.md, LICENSE "staging/$env:ARTIFACT/"
          Compress-Archive -Path "staging/$env:ARTIFACT/*" -DestinationPath "staging/$env:ARTIFACT.zip"

      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v7
        with:
          name: ${{ matrix.artifact }}
          path: staging/${{ matrix.artifact }}.${{ matrix.archive }}
          if-no-files-found: error

  build-tauri:
    name: tauri / ${{ matrix.name }}
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        include:
          - name: macos-universal
            os: macos-latest
            args: --target universal-apple-darwin
            rust-targets: aarch64-apple-darwin,x86_64-apple-darwin
          - name: linux-x86_64
            os: ubuntu-22.04
            args: ""
            rust-targets: ""
          - name: windows-x86_64
            os: windows-latest
            args: ""
            rust-targets: ""
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
      - uses: jdx/mise-action@v4
        with:
          experimental: true
          install: true
          cache: true
      - name: add rust targets
        if: matrix.rust-targets != ''
        shell: bash
        env:
          RUST_TARGETS: ${{ matrix.rust-targets }}
        run: |
          IFS=',' read -r -a targets <<< "$RUST_TARGETS"
          for t in "${targets[@]}"; do rustup target add "$t"; done
      - uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
        with:
          workspaces: "crates/orpheus-tauri/src-tauri -> target"
          shared-key: latest-tauri-${{ matrix.name }}

      - uses: ./.github/actions/install-linux-deps

      - run: mise run web:install
      - uses: tauri-apps/tauri-action@v0
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        with:
          projectPath: crates/orpheus-tauri/src-tauri
          args: ${{ matrix.args }}

      - name: collect tauri bundles
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          mkdir -p tauri-output
          # Cargo workspace: bundles land under the workspace root `target/`.
          # Layout varies by target triple:
          #   native   -> target/release/bundle/<kind>/...
          #   crossed  -> target/<triple>/release/bundle/<kind>/...
          #               (e.g. target/universal-apple-darwin/release/bundle/...)
          # So search the whole target/ tree and filter by the
          # .../release/bundle/ path segment. Tauri v2 emits duplicates
          # (Orpheus_0.1.0_amd64.deb AND lowercase
          # orpheus_0.1.0_amd64.deb from the Cargo crate name); the
          # Orpheus_/Orpheus- name filter keeps only the productName-cased
          # ones.
          find target -type f -path "*/release/bundle/*" \
            \( -name "Orpheus_*.dmg"    -o -name "Orpheus*.app.tar.gz" \
               -o -name "Orpheus_*.AppImage" \
               -o -name "Orpheus_*.deb" -o -name "Orpheus-*.rpm" \
               -o -name "Orpheus_*.msi" -o -name "Orpheus_*.exe" \) \
            -exec cp {} tauri-output/ \;
          ls -la tauri-output/
          if [ -z "$(ls -A tauri-output/)" ]; then
            echo "::error::no Tauri bundles found under target/**/release/bundle"
            find target -type d -name bundle 2>&1 | head -20 || true
            exit 1
          fi

      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v7
        with:
          name: tauri-${{ matrix.name }}
          path: tauri-output/*
          if-no-files-found: error

  publish-latest:
    name: publish latest pre-release
    needs: [build-cli, build-tauri]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write
    env:
      SHA: ${{ github.sha }}
      RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - uses: actions/download-artifact@v8
        with:
          path: dist
          merge-multiple: true

      - name: list artifacts
        run: ls -la dist/

      - name: sign artifacts with gpg
        uses: ./.github/actions/sign-artifacts
        with:
          artifacts-dir: dist
          gpg-private-key: ${{ secrets.GPG_PRIVATE_KEY }}
          gpg-passphrase: ${{ secrets.GPG_PASSPHRASE }}
          gpg-key-id: ${{ secrets.GPG_KEY_ID }}

      - name: delete previous latest release
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        run: gh release delete "$LATEST_TAG" --yes --cleanup-tag || true

      - name: create latest pre-release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v3
        with:
          tag_name: ${{ env.LATEST_TAG }}
          name: "Latest (main @ ${{ github.sha }})"
          prerelease: true
          make_latest: "false"
          body: |
            **Rolling pre-release built from `main`.** Do not depend on it for
            reproducible builds — it changes on every push. For a stable
            version, use a tagged release.

            - commit: `${{ github.sha }}`
            - run:    `${{ github.run_id }}`

            All artifacts are GPG-signed. Verify with:
            ```
            gpg --verify SHA256SUMS.asc SHA256SUMS
            sha256sum -c SHA256SUMS
            ```
          files: dist/*
          fail_on_unmatched_files: true

These templates are rendered by `.github/workflows/publish-cask.yml` into
the external tap repo `dougnukem/homebrew-orpheus` on each tagged release.

- `orpheus.rb.universal.template` — used when a single universal DMG

  output of `tauri build --target universal-apple-darwin`.

## Bootstrapping the tap

The first time, create the tap repo by hand:

```bash
gh repo create dougnukem/homebrew-orpheus --public --add-readme \
  --description "Homebrew tap for Orpheus"
cd homebrew-orpheus
mkdir Casks
# seed with a placeholder the workflow will overwrite
printf 'cask "orpheus" do\n  version "0.0.0"\nend\n' > Casks/orpheus.rb
git add . && git commit -m "init tap" && git push
```

Users then install with:

```bash
brew tap dougnukem/orpheus
brew install --cask orpheus
```

Once Apple Developer signing is wired up in `release.yml`, delete the
`caveats <<~EOS ... EOS` block in both templates. The cask will then
install and launch cleanly with no manual `xattr` step.

# Homebrew cask templates

These templates are rendered into the external tap repo
`dougnukem/homebrew-orpheus` by two workflows:

- `.github/workflows/publish-cask.yml` — fires on tagged releases and
  writes `Casks/orpheus.rb` from one of the stable templates below.
- `.github/workflows/publish-cask-head.yml` — fires from `latest.yml`
  on every push to `main` and writes `Casks/orpheus-head.rb` from the
  HEAD template.

Templates:

- `orpheus.rb.universal.template` — stable cask, used when a single
  universal DMG (`Orpheus_<ver>_universal.dmg`) is present on the
  release. This is the output of `tauri build --target
  universal-apple-darwin`.
- `orpheus.rb.perarch.template` — stable cask fallback when
  per-architecture DMGs (`Orpheus_<ver>_aarch64.dmg`,
  `Orpheus_<ver>_x64.dmg`) are present instead. Useful if the universal
  target is disabled.
- `orpheus-head.rb.template` — rolling HEAD cask. Uses `version :latest`
  with `sha256 :no_check` because the DMG contents change on every push to
  `main` without a version bump. Users opt in explicitly via the
  `-head` suffix and re-pull with `brew reinstall --cask orpheus-head`.
  `conflicts_with` prevents co-installing the stable cask.

Token substitution:

| Token              | Meaning                                             |
|--------------------|-----------------------------------------------------|
| `%%VERSION%%`      | Release version without the leading `v` (e.g. `0.1.0`) |
| `%%REPO%%`         | `<owner>/<repo>` of the app repo                    |
| `%%UNIVERSAL_SHA%%`| SHA-256 of the universal DMG                        |
| `%%ARM_SHA%%`      | SHA-256 of the arm64 DMG                            |
| `%%INTEL_SHA%%`    | SHA-256 of the x86_64 DMG                           |
| `%%DMG_FILENAME%%` | Actual universal DMG filename on the `latest` release (detected at publish time) |
| `%%TAP_OWNER%%`    | Tap GitHub org/user (e.g. `dougnukem`)              |
| `%%TAP_NAME%%`     | Tap short name (e.g. `orpheus` — without `homebrew-` prefix) |

## Bootstrapping the tap

The first time, create the tap repo by hand:

```bash
gh repo create dougnukem/homebrew-orpheus --public --add-readme \
  --description "Homebrew tap for Orpheus"
cd homebrew-orpheus
mkdir Casks
# seed with a placeholder the workflow will overwrite
printf 'cask "orpheus" do\n  version "0.0.0"\nend\n' > Casks/orpheus.rb
git add . && git commit -m "init tap" && git push
```

Users then install with:

```bash
brew tap dougnukem/orpheus
brew install --cask orpheus
```

## Removing the Gatekeeper caveat

Once Apple Developer signing is wired up in `release.yml`, delete the
`caveats <<~EOS ... EOS` block in both templates. The cask will then
install and launch cleanly with no manual `xattr` step.

Adds a second cask (`orpheus-head`) to the `dougnukem/homebrew-orpheus`
tap that tracks the rolling `latest` pre-release on GitHub. Users can:

- `packaging/homebrew/orpheus-head.rb.template` uses `version :latest`
  + `sha256 :no_check` (hash changes on every push; no version bump).
  `conflicts_with` prevents co-installing the stable `orpheus` cask.

  reusable workflow that locates the universal DMG on the `latest`
  release, renders the template into the tap repo, and commits only
  when the cask content actually changed.
- `latest.yml` invokes it via `secrets: inherit` after `publish-latest`
  succeeds so every push to main refreshes the head cask.
- `packaging/homebrew/README.md` documents the new template, tokens,
  and publish flow.

All `${{ github.* }}` context values are routed through `env:` blocks
and used only as shell variables, matching the injection-safe pattern
established by the rest of the CI.
